data.title: Bitcoin Holds $71,000 As Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin Test Breakout Levels
data.description: Bitcoin remains steady around $71,000 amid positive market sentiment in the cryptocurrency space. Bitcoin ETFs experienced a net outflow of $66.7 million on Tuesday, while Ethereum ETFs saw $40.8 million in net outflows. The current prices of major cryptocurrencies are: Bitcoin at $71,846, Ethereum at $2,195.01, Solana at $92.56, XRP at $1.42, Dogecoin at $0.09687, and Shiba Inu at $0.056186. Additionally, Meme coin market capitalization saw a 1.3% increase in the last 24 hours. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Intersand achieves B Corp™ certification, confirming its excellence in sustainability and manufacturing Innovation
data.description: The Intersand Canada Inc. Group (Intersand), a leading manufacturer of advanced cat litters such as OdourLock™ and OdourBuster™, proudly revealed its attainment of B Corp™ certification on March 25, 2026, in Boucherville, QC. This certification highlights the company's commitment to meeting rigorous standards of social and environmental performance, transparency, and accountability.

data.title: CRV aims for $0.25 resistance by April 2026, currently trading at $0.23 with RSI at 47.07. MACD signals caution.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Curve (CRV) is currently trading at a price of $0.23, with a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 47.07. Analysts predict a possible uptick to the $0.25 resistance mark based on technical indicators. However, the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicator shows bearish signals, indicating a need for caution for traders interested in the decentralized finance token.

data.title: Why Velo3D Shares Are Sliding Wednesday?
data.description: Velo3D Inc (NASDAQ: VELO) saw a decline in shares on Wednesday following the release of their fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on Tuesday. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $9.44 million, exceeding expectations of $8.68 million. Their adjusted loss per share was 54 cents, slightly better than the anticipated 56 cents loss. 3D printer and parts revenue dropped by 5% compared to the previous year, attributed to product mix and system sales volume. System sales are expected to be the main revenue driver in 2026. Velo3D's backlog stood at $31 million by the end of the year, with CEO Arun Jeldi highlighting a record for bookings in the quarter. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Pew Policy Tool Shows States Taking Action to Boost Distributed Energy Resources
data.description: Between 2021 and 2025, state legislatures nationwide made significant progress in promoting localized energy solutions to address the increasing pressure on the grid. In Washington, as of March 25, 2026, Brian Watts, a member of The Pew Charitable Trusts' energy modernization initiative, emphasized the rising demand for energy in the United States.

data.title: TÜRKÇİMENTO calls for EU-aligned MRV data recognition as default values under CBAM
data.description: In Ankara, Turkey, on March 25, 2026, the Turkish Cement Manufacturers' Association (TÜRKÇİMENTO) released a strategic statement regarding the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) after the European Commission's latest legislative decision. The association highlighted key figures and data pertaining to the impact of the CBAM on Turkey's cement industry, emphasizing the need for proactive measures to address potential challenges posed by this mechanism.

data.title: NVIDIA reports 1M X efficiency gains over 6 GPU gens, with Vera Rubin platform offering 10x higher inference throughput/MW for AI.
data.description: NVIDIA has revealed information on the Vera Rubin platform, which achieves a significant increase in inference throughput per megawatt, reaching up to 10 times higher performance. This advancement is expected to have a transformative impact on the economics of AI data centers and revenue models for token factories.
2025 annual report released, showing revenue of $10 million, a 15% increase from last year.

data.title: Liberty Energy Inc. Announces Proposed $450.0 Million Convertible Senior Notes Offering
data.description: Denver-based company Liberty Energy Inc. (NYSE: LBRT) has revealed plans to offer $450.0 million in convertible senior notes due 2032 (the “Notes”), subject to market conditions. The private offering (the “Notes Offering”) will be made to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A of the Securities Act of 1933. Liberty aims to also provide initial rights for the purchasers to buy up to an additional $67.5 million principal amount of Notes.

data.title: BrightView’s Sports and Golf Portfolios Continue to Expand
data.description: BrightView, a top commercial landscaping company traded on the NYSE under the ticker symbol BV, is significantly growing its Sports and Golf divisions. The company's impressive work is being featured at prominent sporting events nationwide and internationally. BrightView is known for its proficiency in managing renowned venues and competitions, including Major League Baseball's Field of Dreams and London Series, as well as FIFA World Cup and Major League Soccer grounds, among others. The company's success in these sectors is solidifying its position as a leader in the industry.

data.title: NUBURU Activates U.S. Defense Manufacturing Infrastructure, Initiates Phase I Execution with Maddox Defense
data.description: In Denver, NUBURU, Inc. (NYSE American: BURU), specializing in non-kinetic effects, directed-energy technologies, electronic warfare, and defense systems, has initiated Phase I of its joint venture with Maddox Defense Incorporated. The venture, through Nuburu Defense LLC, signifies a pivotal move in the Company's expansion into U.S. defense.
